label: Unit costs of public service outcomes for VfM evaluation
description: Curated subset of the Greater Manchester Combined Authority (GMCA) Unit Cost Database, adopted as Green Book supplementary guidance in 2014. These unit costs represent the average fiscal and economic cost of outcomes across public services. Used to monetise the benefits of interventions that prevent negative outcomes or generate positive ones.

reoffending_event.label: Average cost per reoffending event
reoffending_event.includes: Police, courts, legal aid, probation, prison/community sentence, victim costs

domestic_violence_incident.label: Cost per domestic violence incident
domestic_violence_incident.includes: Criminal justice, health, social services, housing, lost output, human costs

prison_place_per_year.label: Cost per prison place per year

anti_social_behaviour_incident.label: Cost per ASB incident to public services

youth_crime_incident.label: Average cost per youth crime incident

fiscal_benefit_employment.label: Annual fiscal benefit of moving one person from unemployment to employment
fiscal_benefit_employment.includes: Tax revenue gained, benefits no longer paid
fiscal_benefit_employment.note: Based on median full-time earnings. Higher for higher-wage jobs.

cost_of_unemployment_individual.label: Annual cost of unemployment per person (wider economic cost)
cost_of_unemployment_individual.includes: Lost output, benefit payments, health impacts, reduced wellbeing

jsa_uc_annual_cost.label: Annual cost of JSA/UC standard allowance per claimant

neet_cost_per_year.label: Annual cost per young person not in education, employment, or training (NEET)

apprenticeship_fiscal_return.label: Estimated lifetime fiscal return per apprenticeship completion
apprenticeship_fiscal_return.note: Varies significantly by level and sector

hospital_admission.label: Average cost per non-elective hospital admission

gp_consultation.label: Cost per GP consultation
gp_consultation.source_detail: PSSRU Unit Costs of Health and Social Care 2024

mental_health_inpatient_day.label: Cost per mental health inpatient bed day

substance_misuse_treatment.label: Average cost per substance misuse treatment episode

qaly.label: Value of a Quality-Adjusted Life Year
qaly.note: Cross-referenced with uk/health-values.json. GBP 70,000 is the Green Book cross-government value (2019 prices). Distinct from NICE's clinical threshold of GBP 25,000-35,000/QALY. Uprate using GDP deflator for other price years.

wellby.label: Value of a Wellbeing-Adjusted Life Year (WELLBY)
wellby.note: Green Book supplementary guidance on wellbeing (2021). One WELLBY = one point on a 0-10 life satisfaction scale sustained for one year. Use for monetising subjective wellbeing impacts where QALY is not appropriate.
wellby.source_detail: HM Treasury Wellbeing Guidance for Appraisal (2021)

smoking_cessation_qaly_gain.label: Average QALY gain per successful smoking cessation
smoking_cessation_qaly_gain.note: Multiply by QALY value for monetised benefit

excluded_pupil_lifetime.label: Estimated lifetime cost per permanently excluded pupil
excluded_pupil_lifetime.includes: Alternative provision, NEET costs, reduced earnings, crime

level_2_qualification_lifetime.label: Lifetime value of achieving a Level 2 qualification (vs none)
level_2_qualification_lifetime.includes: Higher earnings, tax revenue, reduced benefit dependence

level_3_qualification_lifetime.label: Lifetime value of achieving a Level 3 qualification (vs Level 2)

degree_lifetime_premium.label: Estimated lifetime earnings premium of a degree (vs Level 3)
degree_lifetime_premium.note: Varies significantly by subject and institution

school_absence_day.label: Estimated cost per day of school absence
school_absence_day.note: Economic cost (reduced attainment, supervision). Not direct fiscal cost.

homelessness_per_year.label: Annual cost per homeless person to public services
homelessness_per_year.includes: Temporary accommodation, health, social services, criminal justice

rough_sleeping_per_year.label: Annual cost per rough sleeper to public services
rough_sleeping_per_year.note: Higher than general homelessness due to acute health and policing costs

temporary_accommodation_per_year.label: Average cost of temporary accommodation per household per year

social_housing_tenancy_annual_saving.label: Annual fiscal saving from moving household from TA to social housing
social_housing_tenancy_annual_saving.note: Difference in housing benefit costs + reduced churn costs

eviction_cost.label: Average cost of an eviction (all parties)
eviction_cost.includes: Legal, court, enforcement, temporary accommodation placement

child_in_care_per_year.label: Average cost per looked-after child per year

child_protection_plan.label: Annual cost per child on a child protection plan

adult_social_care_residential.label: Annual cost per adult in residential care

adult_social_care_community.label: Annual cost per adult receiving community social care

troubled_families_programme.label: Average annual cost per family in the Supporting Families programme

fire_incident.label: Average cost per fire incident
fire_incident.includes: Fire service response, property damage, injuries, fatalities (where applicable)

usage_guidance: These unit costs enable 'fiscal return' calculations in VfM evaluations. To estimate fiscal savings: count the number of negative outcomes avoided (e.g., 50 fewer reoffending events), multiply by the unit cost (50 x GBP 18,000 = GBP 900,000), and apply additionality adjustments. Compare the resulting fiscal saving to the programme cost to derive a fiscal BCR.

source.publication: Greater Manchester Combined Authority Unit Cost Database (November 2025); NHS National Cost Collection 2023-24; HMPPS Costs per Place 2024-25; PSSRU Unit Costs of Health and Social Care; DfE School Funding Statistics
source.publisher: GMCA; NHS England; HMPPS; PSSRU; DfE
source.update_frequency: GMCA database updated approximately annually. NHS costs updated annually. HMPPS costs updated annually.

caveats: Unit costs are national averages. Actual costs vary significantly by region, local service configuration, and individual circumstances.
Many costs are estimated using the 'cost of consequences' approach (what would it cost if the outcome occurred), not the 'cost of delivery' approach (what does the service cost to run).
Lifetime values (qualifications, employment) involve significant assumptions about persistence, discount rates, and counterfactual earnings.
Avoid double-counting: if a programme prevents homelessness AND reduces crime, check whether the homelessness unit cost already includes a crime component.
These are fiscal and economic costs. They do not capture the full social cost (wellbeing, pain, suffering) unless explicitly noted.
